skill.name: revops
skill.description: When the user wants help with revenue operations, lead lifecycle management, or marketing-to-sales handoff processes. Also use when the user mentions 'RevOps,' 'revenue operations,' 'lead scoring,' 'lead routing,' 'MQL,' 'SQL,' 'pipeline stages,' 'deal desk,' 'CRM automation,' 'marketing-to-sales handoff,' 'data hygiene,' 'leads aren't getting to sales,' 'pipeline management,' 'lead qualification,' or 'when should marketing hand off to sales.' Use this for anything involving the systems and processes that connect marketing to revenue. For cold outreach emails, see cold-email. For email drip campaigns, see email-sequence. For pricing decisions, see pricing-strategy.
skill.summary: Design and optimize revenue operations systems including lead lifecycle management, scoring, routing, and CRM automation.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: This skill contains documentation-only content (markdown files with RevOps frameworks, templates, and process guides). Static analysis detected 54 patterns flagged as potential security issues, but all are false positives. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings reference scoring algorithms in documentation, not actual crypto code. 'External commands' and 'at command' findings are workflow scheduling examples in markdown. 'Path traversal' findings are legitimate relative markdown link references. No executable code, network calls, or filesystem operations exist in this skill.

content.user_title: Optimize revenue operations and lead lifecycle management
content.value_statement: Revenue teams struggle with disconnected systems, undefined handoffs, and poor data hygiene that leak pipeline. This skill provides battle-tested frameworks for lead scoring, routing, CRM automation, and metrics dashboards that align marketing, sales, and customer success.
actual_capabilities: Design complete lead lifecycle stage frameworks with entry/exit criteria and SLAs
Build lead scoring models with explicit, implicit, and negative scoring dimensions
Create lead routing decision trees with territory, ABM, and round-robin assignment
Configure CRM automation workflows for HubSpot, Salesforce, and cross-tool integrations
Define pipeline stage management with required fields and stale deal detection
Specify RevOps metrics dashboards covering conversion rates, velocity, and LTV:CAC
limitations: Does not execute CRM workflows — provides configuration specifications only
Requires user to implement in their actual CRM platform (HubSpot, Salesforce, etc.)
Does not replace dedicated RevOps consultants for complex enterprise implementations
Assumes user has access to a CRM and marketing automation platform

use_cases.title: B2B SaaS startup building first RevOps system
use_cases.description: A 20-person SaaS company using HubSpot needs to define lead stages, set up scoring, and automate MQL handoffs from marketing to their two-person sales team.
use_cases.target_user: Head of Marketing or first RevOps hire

use_cases.title: Mid-market company fixing pipeline hygiene
use_cases.description: A 200-person company with messy CRM data, stale deals, and no stage discipline needs pipeline stage definitions, required field enforcement, and stale deal alerts.
use_cases.target_user: VP Sales or Sales Operations Manager

use_cases.title: Enterprise aligning marketing and sales
use_cases.description: A 1000+ person organization where marketing and sales disagree on MQL definitions needs shared lifecycle stage templates, SLAs, and recycling workflows.
use_cases.target_user: Chief Revenue Officer or RevOps Director

prompt_templates.title: Beginner: Define lead lifecycle stages
prompt_templates.prompt: We're a B2B SaaS company using HubSpot. Our marketing and sales teams disagree on what qualifies as an MQL. Help us define lifecycle stages from subscriber to customer with clear criteria for each transition and SLAs for handoffs.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time RevOps setup for a growing startup

prompt_templates.title: Beginner: Build a lead scoring model
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a lead scoring model for us. We sell mid-market software ($10k ACV). Use fit scoring (company size, title, industry) and engagement scoring (page visits, content downloads, demo requests). Include negative scoring too.

prompt_templates.title: Intermediate: Fix pipeline management issues
prompt_templates.prompt: Our pipeline is a mess — deals sit in stages forever and nobody updates fields. Give us a framework for pipeline stage management with required fields per stage, maximum time-in-stage rules, and automation to enforce hygiene.
prompt_templates.scenario: Cleaning up an existing messy pipeline

prompt_templates.title: Advanced: Complete RevOps system design
prompt_templates.prompt: Design our complete RevOps system. We're a sales-led company ($50k ACV) using Salesforce. Include: lifecycle stage definitions, lead scoring with calibration guidance, routing rules for named accounts and territories, pipeline stage automation, deal desk approval tiers, and a metrics dashboard for our leadership team.
prompt_templates.scenario: Full RevOps overhaul for enterprise company

output_examples.input: Help us set up lead lifecycle stages for our B2B SaaS company using HubSpot.
output: Lifecycle Stage Document with 7 stages: Subscriber, Lead, MQL, SQL, Opportunity, Customer, Evangelist
Entry and exit criteria for each stage with owner assignment
MQL-to-SQL SLA: First contact within 4 business hours, decision within 48 hours
HubSpot workflow specifications for stage transitions and alerts

output_examples.input: Build a lead scoring model for enterprise software ($50k ACV).
output: Explicit scoring: Company size 500+ (+20), VP+ title (+20), target industry (+15), budget authority (+15)
Implicit scoring: Demo request (+30), multiple stakeholders (+20), pricing page 2+ visits (+15)
Negative scoring: Company under 100 employees (-30), individual contributor only (-15), competitor domain (-50)
MQL threshold: 75 points with quarterly recalibration cadence

best_practices: Define stage criteria on paper before automating — automating a broken process creates broken results faster
Set MQL thresholds based on closed-won historical data, not arbitrary numbers — retroactively score past deals to find the natural breakpoint
Enforce required fields at each pipeline stage — block stage advancement if fields are empty to maintain data integrity
anti_patterns: Weighting content downloads too heavily in scoring — research behavior does not equal buying intent
Setting MQL threshold and never recalibrating — buyer behavior changes; recalibrate at least quarterly
Allowing deals to skip pipeline stages — detect and alert when deals jump from Qualified to Proposal without Discovery

faq.question: What's the difference between MQL and SQL?
faq.answer: MQL (Marketing Qualified Lead) means the lead passes fit and engagement thresholds set by marketing. SQL (Sales Qualified Lead) means sales has had a qualifying conversation and confirmed at least two of: budget, authority, need, or timeline. MQL is marketing-to-sales handoff; SQL is sales acceptance.

faq.question: How do we set our MQL scoring threshold?
faq.answer: Pull closed-won data from the last 6-12 months, retroactively score each deal using your model, and find the score that separated wins from losses. Set the threshold just below where 80% of closed-won deals would have scored. Validate against closed-lost deals.

faq.question: What's a good speed-to-lead response time?
faq.answer: Under 5 minutes is the gold standard — leads contacted within 5 minutes are 21x more likely to qualify. After 30 minutes, conversion drops 10x. Build automation to alert reps instantly and escalate if SLA is missed.

faq.question: How often should we recalibrate our lead scoring model?
faq.answer: PLG/high-volume companies should recalibrate monthly. Mid-market and enterprise should recalibrate quarterly. Warning signs you need recalibration: MQL-to-SQL acceptance rate below 30%, sales consistently rejects MQLs, or high-scoring leads don't convert.

faq.question: What metrics should be on our RevOps dashboard?
faq.answer: Full-funnel metrics: Lead-to-MQL rate (5-15%), MQL-to-SQL rate (30-50%), SQL-to-Opportunity (50-70%), win rate (20-30%), pipeline velocity, LTV:CAC ratio (target 3:1+), speed-to-lead (target under 5 minutes), and CAC payback period.

faq.question: How do we handle recycled leads after sales rejection?
faq.answer: Assign rejection reason codes (FIT-01 for company too small, ENG-01 for no response, etc.). Leads enter a recycling nurture at lower frequency. Reset engagement score to baseline but keep fit score. If lead re-engages and crosses MQL threshold again, re-route with a 'Recycled MQL' flag and track separately.
